title: Hegseth gives Anthropic CEO until Friday to back down in AI safeguards fight

title: Former Iraq PM Maliki vows to pursue return to office despite US opposition
description:
Former Iraqi Prime Minister Nouri al Maliki has vowed to continue pursuing a return to office regardless of US pressure opposing his candidacy, intensifying political uncertainty following Iraq’s November 2025 elections. Maliki’s bid comes as Shiite factions within the Coordination Framework weigh their options amid ongoing efforts to select a new president and prime minister.

title: US State Department orders non-emergency personnel to depart from Beirut embassy
description:
The US State Department ordered the departure of all non-emergency government personnel from the US Embassy in Beirut, along with their family members. Many media outlets tied this to rising US regional tensions with Iran. A Lebanese media outlet suggested it was in response to threats to the embassy from Hezbollah.

title: Ukraine allies scramble on anniversary date: Is it Europe's war now?

title: Marking four years of war in Ukraine with powerful music and films
description: In this episode of arts24, we mark four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine with powerful films from the frontlines. The BAFTA-winning documentary "Mr. Nobody Against Putin" exposes how Russian schools have been used for propaganda, while Ukrainian filmmaker Mstyslav Chernov captures frontline courage in "2000 Meters to Andriivka", which follows soldiers during the 2023 counteroffensive.

title: Senegal's PM introduces bill doubling prison sentences for same-sex relations
description: Senegalese Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko announced that he had introduced a bill toughening penalties for same-sex relations and making them punishable by five to 10 years in prison amid a wave of homophobia and arrests in the West African country.

title: Ukraine war: Brazil 'seems to want to project an image of not taking sides'
description: While Europe and the US have largely backed Kyiv with sanctions and weapons, countries in the Global South have tried to tow a more neutral line, none more so than Brazil. The country’s president Lula has condemned the invasion in principle, but he also refused to take sides. Our correspondent in Brazil, Jan Onoszko, tells us more.

title: French President Macron accepts the resignation of Louvre director Laurence des Cars
description: The head of Paris's Louvre museum Laurence des Cars on Tuesday handed in her resignation to French President Emmanuel Macron, who accepted it, hailing it as an “act of responsibility”. The museum, shaken by a high-profile heist last October, is in need of a “new impetus”, Macron's office said.

The World Bank estimates it will take $588 billion over the next 10 years to make up for damage and destruction incurred during the conflict. The housing, transport and energy sectors have seen the biggest losses. Also in this edition: Panama takes control of two key ports on the Panama Canal.

title: 'They look like a toy': Ukrainian civilians under threat from ‘petal mines’

Also known as "petal mines", these mines are used by both sides, and can be scattered by rockets, bombs, and, increasingly, drones. Designed to cause injury rather than kill outright, they are, however, particularly dangerous for civilians because they are hard to identify – and to defuse. title: Britain's former US ambassador Peter Mandelson arrested amid Epstein revelations
description: London police arrested former UK ambassador to the US Peter Mandelson on Monday as part of a misconduct probe over his ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Emails released by the US Department of Justice in January appeared to show Mandelson sharing information with the disgraced financier while a minister in then-premier Gordon Brown's cabinet.

title: Blizzard warnings, travel bans in effect as brutal storm pounds Northeast
description: Blizzard warnings blanket the Northeast Monday as a beastly winter storm is snarling travel and pounding millions with heavy snow.Threat level: In hard-hit areas, travel will be "extremely treacherous," forecasters say, and some leaders are restricting it. title: U.S.-Iran talks expected Thursday to discuss nuclear proposal
description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators are expected to meet in Geneva on Thursday to discuss a detailed Iranian proposal for a nuclear deal. A senior U.S. official told Axios on Sunday morning the Trump administration expects to receive the proposal by Tuesday. title: Russian MOD Shows U.S. F-35, F-16 Intercepting Tu-95MS And Su-35S Off Alaska
description: The intercept was carried out as the Russian Tu-95MS bombers and their escorting fighters transited the Alaskan ADIZ (Air Defense Identification Zone). On Feb. 19, 2026, the U.S. Air Force launched two F-16s, two F-35s, one E-3, and four KC-135s tankers to intercept, positively identify, and escort the aircraft until they departed the Alaskan ADIZ. title: Sweden orders eight Seasnake 30 systems from Rheinmetall
description:
Sweden has contracted German defence company Rheinmetall to provide eight Seasnake 30 weapon systems for use by the Swedish Navy.

title: The revamp of Ireland’s defence
description:
Despite a long-standing policy of neutrality, Ireland has recognised Russia as a threat, and is following Europe in rearmament.

title: Israel strikes Hamas in Lebanon, Palestinian terrorists violate Gaza ceasefire 14 times from February 5 to 20, IDF says
description:
As US President Donald Trump convened the Gaza Board of Peace for its inaugural meeting in Washington, DC, the IDF continued to report numerous ceasefire violations in the territory. Israel also struck the Ain al Hilweh Palestinian refugee camp near Sidon, Lebanon, stating that the target was used for the “preparation of terrorist activities against IDF forces in Lebanese territory.”

title: Stabilization force and funding pledged for Gaza at Board of Peace meeting
description: At a February 19 meeting of the US-backed Board of Peace in Washington, DC, five countries pledged troops to an International Stabilization Force for Gaza, and representatives of 40 nations committed approximately $7 billion in funding. The initiative is part of the second phase of a ceasefire plan launched in October 2025. title: Israeli operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah: February 9–15, 2026
description:
Israel continued military operations in Lebanon between February 9 and February 15, 2026. Israeli activities last week were concentrated in south Lebanon and consisted largely of policing activity, maintaining the reduced intensity of previous weeks.

title: Syrian government and SDF continue transition, US strikes Islamic State and withdraws from Tanf
description: The Syrian government and the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) have continued implementing a January transition agreement. US Central Command has maintained pressure on the Islamic State with airstrikes and withdrew from the Tanf garrison in southern Syria. title: Hamas renews threats against rival Palestinian militias in Gaza
description:
Hamas warned opposing militias that their days were numbered and that the Israeli military could not protect them. The threat came after some militias have carried out successful operations against Hamas in recent weeks and months.

title: Israeli operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah: February 2–8, 2026
description:
Israel continued military operations in Lebanon between February 2 and February 8, 2026. Israeli activities last week were concentrated in south Lebanon and significantly less intense and numerous than in preceding weeks.

title: Turkish foreign minister warns of nuclear arms race if Iran gets the bomb
description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan warned on February 9 that Ankara may pursue nuclear weapons if Iran acquires them, signaling a potential shift from rhetorical grievance to a conditional proliferation policy. title: Terror concerns as Ukraine-Russia war hits four-year mark
description: An attack in the western city of Lviv has raised concerns over the prospect of a growing terror threat in Ukrainian cities.

title: UK joins European air defence project despite DIP hold-up
description: The UK has joined a pan-European air defence programme known as LEAP, intended to detect and intercept hostile small drones.

title: Generation Jihad | The West’s greatest threat is still Al Qaeda
description: In its latest report on the state of global jihad, the UN reveals that Al Qaeda is expanding but doesn't mention one critical fact: the jihadist group's leader is based in Iran.
